name: pdf_tools
short_description: PDF manipulation and Markdown conversion toolkit for extracting page ranges and converting PDF content to Markdown.
long_description: pdf_tools is a Python package that provides utilities for manipulating PDF files and converting their content to Markdown format. It wraps PyPDF2 for PDF operations and MarkItDown for format conversion. The package supports extracting single pages or multiple page ranges from PDFs, counting pages, and converting selected page ranges to Markdown text. All PDF operations are kept in memory when possible to efficiently handle subsets of large files without rewriting the originals. It provides both a programmatic Python API through the PDFTools class and a command-line interface (pdftools) for quick operations.

modules.description: Core PDFTools class providing PDF page operations (counting, extraction, range slicing) and Markdown conversion from PDF pages using in-memory processing.
Command-line interface exposing all PDF tooling functionality through subcommands (count, extract, markdown) with argument parsing and error handling.
Package initializer exporting the main PDFTools class and type aliases (PdfPath, PageRange) for public API access.
Test suite covering page counting, single/multiple range extraction, and Markdown conversion functionality using ReportLab-generated test PDFs.

author: Philip Orange <git@philiporange.com>
